title: parse_int / parse_float dead-duplicate removal in fn call_builtin (12 LOC)
summary: Remove unreachable second `if name == "parse_int"` / `parse_float` branches at L13639-13650 of hexa-lang/self/hexa_full.hexa. Both sites live inside fn call_builtin (declared at L9927) which is a flat dispatch chain — first match returns. The canonical tag-aware version at L13493/13500 (handles TAG_STR / TAG_INT / TAG_FLOAT, idempotent for typed inputs) always wins; the L13639/13645 fallback was naive `.str_val`-only access that would have crashed on non-STR inputs anyway, but was never reached. Pattern mirrors ω-stdlib-8's dead-duplicate sweep for len/to_string/type_of/etc.

predecessors.omega_stdlib_8: earlier dead-duplicate removal in same fn (len/to_string/type_of/int|to_int|to_i64/float|to_float/bool) — established the 'first-match wins' pattern marker comment at L13609-13611

context.problem: Two `if name == "parse_int"` blocks within fn call_builtin (L13493 + L13639) and likewise for parse_float (L13500 + L13645). The flat dispatch chain pattern means the later block can never execute — pure code-debt and a misleading semantic surface (the dead version had buggy logic that would have crashed on INT/FLOAT inputs).
context.decision_path: Audit at the second site (L13639 vs L13493) showed the latter is strictly more capable (5 branches vs 2). Tracked enclosing fn for both via awk fn-tracker → identical (L9927 fn call_builtin). No conditional gate or scope re-entry between them. L10018/10067 are inside `name == "to_int"`/`to_float`' bodies and use .parse_int()/.parse_float() as STRING METHODS, not as named-builtin dispatch — distinct concern, untouched.

files_edited.added: 4-line marker comment in same style as ω-stdlib-8 (L13609-13611), referencing the canonical fast-path L~13493/13500

validation.fixture: parse_int("42"), parse_int("  -7 "), parse_int(99), parse_int(3.7), parse_int(), parse_float("2.71"), parse_float("  -1.5e3 "), parse_float(5), parse_float(2.5), parse_float()
validation.result: all 10 cases match — tag-aware behaviour preserved (STR / INT / FLOAT / empty)

validation.argument: Both removed branches sat inside the same fn call_builtin (L9927, ~4000 LOC). The dispatcher uses the pattern `if name == "X" { ... return ... }` repeated for each builtin. Earliest matching branch returns; later identical-name branches are unreachable. ω-stdlib-8 established and documented this pattern.

scope_constraints_honoured: minimal blast — 12 LOC removed, no semantic change to live behaviour

no parallel-session collision — hexa-lang has uncommitted M on bench/native_gate.c/hexa_v2/escalator.plist, NOT on self/hexa_full.hexa; the .claude/worktrees/agent-a49baa5f/ has its own copy and is isolated by git worktree
CLI-only constraint — N/A (intra-repo edit; no inter-repo invocation involved)
no commit in anima — closure JSON written as untracked

non_changes.L13493_canonical: kept as-is — full tag-aware impl (TAG_STR via .parse_int/.parse_float methods, TAG_INT idempotent, TAG_FLOAT via floor / +0.0 cast, empty-args zero default)
non_changes.L10018_L10067: untouched — these are STRING METHOD calls (.parse_int()/.parse_float()) inside the to_int / to_float dispatch, not name-matching builtin dispatch
